categories.description: Pixar Animation Studios films and characters

categories.description: Disney Princess characters and their stories

categories.name: Mixed/Random
categories.description: A mix of questions from all categories

questions.question: What was the first full-length animated Disney feature film?
options: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
questions.explanation: Snow White released in 1937 was Disney's first animated feature.

questions.question: What year did Beauty and the Beast release?
questions.explanation: Beauty and the Beast released in 1991 and was the first animated film nominated for Best Picture.

questions.question: What type of fish is Nemo?
questions.explanation: Nemo is a clownfish which live among sea anemones.

questions.question: What is the name of the kingdom in Tangled?
questions.explanation: Corona is where Rapunzel was born as a princess.

questions.question: In Frozen what is Kristoff's reindeer's name?
questions.explanation: Sven is Kristoff's loyal reindeer companion.

questions.question: What is the restaurant name in Ratatouille?
questions.explanation: Gusteau's is where Remy helps Linguini cook.

questions.question: In Moana who is the demigod that helps her?
questions.explanation: Maui is the shapeshifting demigod voiced by Dwayne Johnson.

questions.question: In Zootopia what is the sloth's name at the DMV?
questions.explanation: Flash is the hilariously slow sloth at the DMV.

questions.question: What is the name of Woody's horse in Toy Story?
questions.explanation: Bullseye is Woody's loyal horse who appears in Toy Story 2.

questions.question: Which film features the song Circle of Life?
questions.explanation: Circle of Life opens The Lion King as Simba is presented.

questions.question: What was the first Pixar feature film?
questions.explanation: Toy Story released in 1995 was the first fully computer-animated feature film.

questions.question: What is the name of the emotion characters' headquarters in Inside Out?
questions.explanation: Headquarters is where Joy, Sadness, and other emotions work.

questions.question: In Up how many balloons lift Carl's house?
questions.explanation: Pixar calculated it would take about 20,622 balloons.

questions.question: What is the name of the robot in WALL-E that he falls in love with?
questions.explanation: EVE is the sleek probe robot WALL-E falls in love with.

questions.question: In Coco what is the name of the Land of the Dead holiday?
questions.explanation: Dia de los Muertos is the Mexican holiday celebrated in Coco.

questions.question: What company does Mr. Incredible work for as a claims adjuster?
questions.explanation: Insuricare is where Bob Parr works his boring desk job.

questions.question: In Monsters Inc what powers the city?
questions.explanation: Monstropolis is powered by children's screams until they discover laughs work better.

questions.question: What is the Pizza Planet truck?
options: A recurring Easter egg in Pixar films
questions.explanation: The Pizza Planet truck appears in nearly every Pixar film as an Easter egg.

questions.question: What is Baby Yoda's real name?
questions.explanation: Grogu is the real name of the Child revealed in The Mandalorian.

questions.question: What planet is Luke Skywalker from?
questions.explanation: Luke grew up on the desert planet Tatooine with his uncle and aunt.

questions.question: What color is Mace Windu's lightsaber?
questions.explanation: Mace Windu has a unique purple lightsaber requested by Samuel L. Jackson.

questions.question: Who is Kylo Ren's mother?
questions.explanation: Kylo Ren is Ben Solo, son of Leia Organa and Han Solo.

questions.question: What is the name of Han Solo's ship?
questions.explanation: The Millennium Falcon is Han's famous light freighter.

questions.question: Which planet is destroyed by the Death Star in A New Hope?
questions.explanation: Alderaan, Princess Leia's home planet, is destroyed by the Death Star.

questions.question: What is the name of Thor's hammer?
questions.explanation: Mjolnir is Thor's enchanted hammer that only the worthy can lift.

questions.question: What is Tony Stark's superhero name?
questions.explanation: Tony Stark is Iron Man - genius, billionaire, playboy, philanthropist.

questions.question: What planet is Gamora from?
questions.explanation: Gamora is from Zen-Whoberi before Thanos adopted her.

questions.question: What is the name of the fictional metal in Black Panther?
questions.explanation: Vibranium is found in Wakanda and makes up Captain America's shield.

questions.question: Who is the villain in the first Avengers movie?
questions.explanation: Loki leads the Chitauri invasion of New York in The Avengers.

questions.question: What stone is in Vision's forehead?
questions.explanation: The Mind Stone powers Vision and was originally in Loki's scepter.

questions.question: Who is the villain in The Little Mermaid?
questions.explanation: Ursula is the sea witch who tricks Ariel into trading her voice.

questions.question: What is the name of the villain in 101 Dalmatians?
questions.explanation: Cruella de Vil wants to make a fur coat from dalmatian puppies.

questions.question: Who is Simba's evil uncle in The Lion King?
questions.explanation: Scar kills Mufasa and takes over Pride Rock.

questions.question: What villain says 'Off with their heads'?
questions.explanation: The Queen of Hearts from Alice in Wonderland loves ordering beheadings.

questions.question: Who cursed Aurora in Sleeping Beauty?
questions.explanation: Maleficent cursed Aurora to prick her finger on a spindle.

questions.question: What is Jafar's position in Agrabah?
questions.explanation: Jafar is the Royal Vizier who wants to take over Agrabah.

questions.question: Who is the villain in Tangled?
questions.explanation: Mother Gothel kidnapped Rapunzel to use her magical hair.

questions.question: What does Hades rule in Hercules?
questions.explanation: Hades is god of the Underworld and wants to overthrow Zeus.

questions.question: How many official Disney Princesses are there?
questions.explanation: There are 12 official Disney Princesses in the lineup.

questions.question: Who is the first Disney Princess?
questions.explanation: Snow White from 1937 is the first Disney Princess.

questions.question: Which princess has the power to heal with her hair?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel's magical hair can heal injuries and reverse aging.

questions.question: Which princess is not royalty by birth or marriage?
questions.explanation: Mulan is a warrior, not a princess, but is honorary.

questions.question: What is Ariel's father's name?
questions.explanation: King Triton is Ariel's father and ruler of Atlantica.

questions.question: Which princess talks to animals and has forest friends?
questions.explanation: Snow White, Aurora, and Cinderella all communicate with animals.

questions.question: Who is the youngest Disney Princess?
questions.explanation: Snow White is canonically 14 years old, the youngest princess.

questions.question: Which princess cuts her hair in her movie?
questions.explanation: Both Mulan and Rapunzel cut their hair in their films.

questions.question: Who composed the music for The Lion King?
questions.explanation: Hans Zimmer composed the score with songs by Elton John and Tim Rice.

questions.question: Which movie features the song 'A Whole New World'?
questions.explanation: 'A Whole New World' is from Aladdin, sung on the magic carpet ride.

questions.question: Who wrote the songs for Frozen?
questions.explanation: The Lopez duo wrote 'Let It Go' and all Frozen songs.

questions.question: What Disney movie features the song 'Under the Sea'?
questions.explanation: 'Under the Sea' is Sebastian's song in The Little Mermaid.

questions.question: Who composed most Disney Renaissance films like Little Mermaid and Beauty and the Beast?
questions.explanation: Alan Menken composed 8 Disney films including the Renaissance era.

questions.question: Which movie features the song 'How Far I'll Go'?
questions.explanation: 'How Far I'll Go' is Moana's signature song written by Lin-Manuel Miranda.

questions.question: What song does Elsa sing when building her ice palace?
options: Do You Want to Build a Snowman
For the First Time in Forever
questions.explanation: 'Let It Go' plays as Elsa builds her ice palace and embraces her powers.

questions.question: Who wrote the songs for Encanto?
questions.explanation: Lin-Manuel Miranda wrote all songs including 'We Don't Talk About Bruno'.

questions.question: What year did Disneyland open?
questions.explanation: Disneyland opened July 17, 1955 in Anaheim, California.

questions.question: What is the name of the castle at Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Cinderella Castle is the 189-foot icon of Magic Kingdom.

questions.question: What is the Star Wars land called?
questions.explanation: Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge is set on the planet Batuu.

questions.question: How many Disney theme park resorts exist worldwide?
questions.explanation: There are 6 Disney resorts in California, Florida, Tokyo, Paris, Hong Kong, and Shanghai.

questions.question: What is a Hidden Mickey?
options: Mickey shaped design hidden in parks
questions.explanation: Hidden Mickeys are subtle Mickey shapes hidden throughout Disney parks.

questions.question: What is the spherical structure at EPCOT called?
questions.explanation: Spaceship Earth is the iconic geodesic sphere at EPCOT's entrance.

questions.question: Which Disney park is the largest by acreage?
questions.explanation: Animal Kingdom is about 580 acres, the largest Disney park.

questions.question: What is the mine train coaster at Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad is the 'wildest ride in the wilderness'.

questions.question: What famous phrase is at Disneyland entrance?
questions.explanation: 'The Happiest Place on Earth' is Disneyland's famous tagline.

questions.question: What year did Walt Disney World open?
questions.explanation: Magic Kingdom opened October 1, 1971.

questions.question: What was the first Disney cruise ship?
questions.explanation: Disney Magic launched in 1998 as the first Disney cruise ship.

questions.question: What is Disney's private island called?
questions.explanation: Castaway Cay is Disney's private Bahamas island.

questions.question: How many ships are in the Disney Cruise fleet as of 2024?
questions.explanation: Disney has 5 ships: Magic, Wonder, Dream, Fantasy, and Wish.

questions.question: What is the adults only area on Disney ships called?
questions.explanation: Quiet Cove has the adults-only pool and relaxation areas.

questions.question: What unique dining concept does Disney Cruise use?
questions.explanation: Rotational dining means you visit different restaurants but keep your servers.

questions.question: What is the kids club on Disney cruise ships?
questions.explanation: Oceaneer Club is for kids ages 3-12.

questions.question: What is Disney's newest ship as of 2022?
questions.explanation: Disney Wish debuted July 2022.

questions.question: What is the adult beach on Castaway Cay?
questions.explanation: Serenity Bay is the adults-only beach.

questions.question: What does the ship horn play when departing?
options: When You Wish Upon a Star
questions.explanation: Disney ships play 'When You Wish Upon a Star' on departure.

questions.question: What is Pirate Night on Disney Cruise?
questions.explanation: Pirate Night includes dinner, deck party, costumes, and fireworks.

questions.question: What year was Walt Disney born?
questions.explanation: Walt Disney was born December 5, 1901 in Chicago.

questions.question: What was Walt Disney's brother's name?
questions.explanation: Roy O. Disney cofounded Disney with Walt in 1923.

questions.question: What was Mickey Mouse's original name?
questions.explanation: Walt wanted Mortimer, but wife Lillian suggested Mickey.

questions.question: What year did Mickey Mouse debut?
questions.explanation: Mickey debuted in Steamboat Willie on November 18, 1928.

questions.question: Where is Walt Disney buried?
questions.explanation: Walt Disney was cremated and his ashes are at Forest Lawn in Glendale.

questions.question: What was the name of Walt's first successful cartoon character?
questions.explanation: Oswald was created before Mickey, but Walt lost the rights.

questions.question: What was the first Disney Broadway musical?
questions.explanation: Beauty and the Beast opened on Broadway in 1994.

questions.question: What is the longest running Disney Broadway show?
questions.explanation: The Lion King has run since 1997 and is 3rd longest running Broadway show ever.

questions.question: Which Disney Broadway show features puppetry by Julie Taymor?
questions.explanation: Julie Taymor's puppetry and masks are iconic in The Lion King.

questions.question: What Disney movie became a Broadway show in 2014?
questions.explanation: Aladdin opened on Broadway in March 2014.

questions.question: Which show features the song 'Seize the Day'?
questions.explanation: 'Seize the Day' is the Act 1 finale of Newsies.

questions.question: What flavor is the famous Dole Whip?
questions.explanation: Pineapple Dole Whip is the iconic Disney parks frozen treat.

questions.question: What is the bestselling food item at Disney parks?
questions.explanation: Churros are the top seller with millions sold annually.

questions.question: What is the name of the restaurant inside Pirates of the Caribbean at Disneyland?
questions.explanation: Blue Bayou is the atmospheric restaurant inside the ride.

questions.question: What shape are the famous Disney ice cream bars?
questions.explanation: Mickey Premium Ice Cream Bars are shaped like Mickey's head.

questions.question: What is the signature drink at Oga's Cantina in Galaxy's Edge?
questions.explanation: Oga's Cantina has many signature drinks including Blue Milk.

questions.question: What restaurant has the 50s TV dinner theme at Hollywood Studios?
questions.explanation: 50's Prime Time Cafe servers act as family members from the 50s.

questions.question: What does 'Ohana' mean in Lilo and Stitch?
questions.explanation: Ohana means family, and family means nobody gets left behind.

questions.question: Which Disney Princess is based on a real person?
questions.explanation: Pocahontas is based on a real Native American woman from the 1600s.

questions.question: What is Buzz Lightyear's famous catchphrase?
options: You've got a friend in me
questions.explanation: 'To infinity and beyond' is Buzz's iconic catchphrase.

questions.question: Which Disney animated film has both parents alive throughout?
questions.explanation: In Mulan, both parents are present and alive the whole film.

questions.question: What 'A113' refers to in Pixar films?
questions.explanation: A113 is the CalArts classroom where many Pixar animators studied.

questions.question: Who voiced both Darth Vader and Mufasa?
questions.explanation: James Earl Jones' iconic voice brought both characters to life.

questions.question: What year did The Little Mermaid release?
questions.explanation: The Little Mermaid released in 1989 and kicked off the Disney Renaissance.

questions.question: What is the name of Simba's childhood friend in The Lion King?
questions.explanation: Nala is Simba's childhood friend who later becomes his queen.

questions.question: In Aladdin, how many wishes does the Genie grant?
questions.explanation: The Genie grants three wishes but Aladdin uses his last wish to free the Genie.

questions.question: What is the name of the fairy in Peter Pan?
questions.explanation: Tinker Bell is Peter Pan's loyal fairy companion.

questions.question: Who voices Elsa in Frozen?
questions.explanation: Idina Menzel voices Elsa and performs the hit song Let It Go.

questions.question: In The Jungle Book, what type of animal is Baloo?
questions.explanation: Baloo is a fun-loving bear who teaches Mowgli the Bare Necessities.

questions.question: What is the name of Mulan's dragon companion?
questions.explanation: Mushu is a small red dragon voiced by Eddie Murphy who helps Mulan.

questions.question: In Bambi, what type of animal is Thumper?
questions.explanation: Thumper is Bambi's rabbit friend named for his habit of thumping his foot.

questions.question: What is Pocahontas's raccoon friend's name?
questions.explanation: Meeko is the mischievous raccoon who accompanies Pocahontas.

questions.question: Which Disney film features Experiment 626?
questions.explanation: Stitch's official experiment number is 626, created by Dr. Jumba.

questions.question: In Encanto, what is Luisa's magical gift?
questions.explanation: Luisa Madrigal has the gift of super strength and sings Surface Pressure.

questions.question: What is the name of the kingdom in Frozen?
questions.explanation: Arendelle is the Scandinavian-inspired kingdom where Elsa and Anna live.

questions.question: In Tarzan, who raises Tarzan as her own?
questions.explanation: Kala is the gorilla who adopts and raises baby Tarzan as her son.

questions.question: What year did Frozen release?
questions.explanation: Frozen released in November 2013 and became one of Disney's highest-grossing films.

questions.question: In The Princess and the Frog, what is Tiana's dream?
questions.explanation: Tiana dreams of opening her own restaurant in New Orleans.

questions.question: What is the name of the clownfish father in Finding Nemo?
questions.explanation: Marlin is Nemo's overprotective father who crosses the ocean to find him.

questions.question: What is the name of the rat protagonist in Ratatouille?
questions.explanation: Remy is the rat who dreams of becoming a chef in Paris.

questions.question: In Cars, what number is Lightning McQueen?
questions.explanation: Lightning McQueen's number 95 references Toy Story's 1995 release year.

questions.question: What is the name of the lead emotion in Inside Out?
questions.explanation: Joy is the lead emotion who tries to keep Riley happy.

questions.question: In Finding Dory, what kind of fish is Dory?
questions.explanation: Dory is a blue tang with short-term memory loss.

questions.question: What is one of Woody's famous pull-string phrases in Toy Story?
options: There's a snake in my boot
questions.explanation: There's a snake in my boot is one of Woody's most recognizable pull-string phrases.

questions.question: In Brave, what does Merida's mother turn into?
questions.explanation: Queen Elinor is transformed into a bear by a witch's spell.

questions.question: What is the name of the prospector toy villain in Toy Story 2?
questions.explanation: Stinky Pete the Prospector is the villain who was never sold from a store shelf.

questions.question: In The Incredibles, what is Violet's superpower?
questions.explanation: Violet Parr can turn invisible and create protective force fields.

questions.question: What Pixar film is set in the Land of the Dead?
questions.explanation: Coco follows Miguel to the colorful Land of the Dead on Dia de los Muertos.

questions.question: In Soul, what instrument does Joe Gardner play?
questions.explanation: Joe Gardner is a middle school band teacher and jazz pianist.

questions.question: In Turning Red, what does Mei transform into?
questions.explanation: Meilin Lee transforms into a giant red panda whenever she gets emotional.

questions.question: In Luca, what are Luca and Alberto secretly?
questions.explanation: Luca and Alberto are sea monsters who take human form on land.

questions.question: What club does Russell belong to in Up?
questions.explanation: Russell is a Wilderness Explorer working to earn his Assisting the Elderly badge.

questions.question: In Elemental, what are the two main elements that fall in love?
questions.explanation: Ember (fire) and Wade (water) fall in love despite being opposite elements.

questions.question: What is the name of the teddy bear villain in Toy Story 3?
questions.explanation: Lots-o'-Huggin' Bear is a strawberry-scented teddy bear who runs Sunnyside Daycare.

questions.question: What is the name of the old man in Up?
questions.explanation: Carl Fredricksen ties thousands of balloons to his house to fly to Paradise Falls.

questions.question: Who is Luke Skywalker's father?
questions.explanation: Darth Vader is Anakin Skywalker, Luke's father, revealed in The Empire Strikes Back.

questions.question: What is the name of Han Solo's Wookiee co-pilot?
questions.explanation: Chewbacca is Han Solo's loyal Wookiee co-pilot and best friend.

questions.question: What is the weapon of choice for a Jedi?
questions.explanation: The lightsaber is described as an elegant weapon for a more civilized age.

questions.question: Who trained Luke Skywalker on Dagobah?
questions.explanation: Yoda trained Luke in the ways of the Force on the swamp planet Dagobah.

questions.question: What is the name of Boba Fett's father?
questions.explanation: Jango Fett is Boba's father and the template for the Republic's clone army.

questions.question: In what year did the original Star Wars film release?
questions.explanation: Star Wars (A New Hope) premiered on May 25, 1977.

questions.question: Which planet is the home of the Ewoks?
questions.explanation: The forest moon of Endor is home to the Ewoks who help defeat the Empire.

questions.question: Who is the Sith villain in The Phantom Menace?
questions.explanation: Darth Maul is the Sith apprentice with a distinctive double-bladed lightsaber.

questions.question: What planet is the Jedi Temple located on?
questions.explanation: The Jedi Temple is on Coruscant, the capital of the Galactic Republic.

questions.question: What is the name of Boba Fett's ship?
questions.explanation: Slave I is Boba Fett's ship that tracks Han Solo to Cloud City.

questions.question: Who says the famous line 'I am your father'?
questions.explanation: Darth Vader reveals he is Luke's father in The Empire Strikes Back.

questions.question: What type of trooper wears white armor in the Empire?
questions.explanation: Stormtroopers are the iconic white-armored soldiers of the Galactic Empire.

questions.question: What is unique about Kylo Ren's lightsaber?
questions.explanation: Kylo Ren wields a unique crossguard lightsaber with unstable side vents.

questions.question: What is the Mandalorian's real name?
questions.explanation: Din Djarin is the Mandalorian bounty hunter who adopts Grogu.

questions.question: What is Order 66?
options: A command to destroy the Jedi
questions.explanation: Order 66 was Palpatine's command for clone troopers to execute the Jedi.

questions.question: What is the ice planet in The Empire Strikes Back?
questions.explanation: Hoth is the frozen planet where the Rebel Alliance established Echo Base.

questions.question: Who is Rey's grandfather?
questions.explanation: Rey is revealed to be the granddaughter of Emperor Palpatine in The Rise of Skywalker.

questions.question: What is Luke's call sign during the Death Star attack?
questions.explanation: Luke's call sign was Red Five during the Battle of Yavin.

questions.question: What is the name of Han Solo's son?
questions.explanation: Ben Solo is the son of Han and Leia who becomes Kylo Ren.

questions.question: What is Captain America's shield made of?
questions.explanation: Captain America's shield is made of vibranium from Wakanda.

questions.question: What is the name of Tony Stark's AI assistant?
questions.explanation: JARVIS (Just A Rather Very Intelligent System) is Tony's AI who later becomes Vision.

questions.question: Who snaps the Infinity Gauntlet to defeat Thanos in Endgame?
questions.explanation: Tony Stark sacrifices himself by snapping the Infinity Stones to defeat Thanos.

questions.question: What is the name of Thor's home realm?
questions.explanation: Asgard is the home realm of Thor and the Asgardian gods.

questions.question: Who is the Winter Soldier?
questions.explanation: Bucky Barnes is Steve Rogers' childhood friend who was brainwashed into the Winter Soldier.

questions.question: What country is Black Panther the king of?
questions.explanation: T'Challa is the king of Wakanda, a technologically advanced African nation.

questions.question: How many Infinity Stones are there?
questions.explanation: There are six Infinity Stones: Space, Mind, Reality, Power, Time, and Soul.

questions.question: What is Hawkeye's real name?
questions.explanation: Clint Barton is Hawkeye, the expert archer and founding Avenger.

questions.question: What is the name of the raccoon in Guardians of the Galaxy?
questions.explanation: Rocket is the genetically enhanced raccoon and weapons expert.

questions.question: What does Groot always say?
questions.explanation: Groot can only say 'I am Groot' but each time it means something different.

questions.question: Who created Ultron in the MCU?
questions.explanation: Tony Stark and Bruce Banner created Ultron as a peacekeeping AI that went rogue.

questions.question: What artifact does Doctor Strange wear that contains the Time Stone?
questions.explanation: The Eye of Agamotto is the pendant that houses the Time Stone.

questions.question: What is Ant-Man's real name in the MCU?
questions.explanation: Scott Lang is the MCU's Ant-Man, a former thief turned superhero.

questions.question: Who is the main villain in Black Panther?
questions.explanation: Erik Killmonger challenges T'Challa for the throne of Wakanda.

questions.question: What color is the Power Stone?
questions.explanation: The Power Stone is purple and was first seen in Guardians of the Galaxy.

questions.question: Who wields the Stormbreaker axe?
questions.explanation: Thor forges Stormbreaker in Nidavellir to fight Thanos in Infinity War.

questions.question: Who is Peter Parker's aunt?
questions.explanation: Aunt May raises Peter Parker and is played by Marisa Tomei in the MCU.

questions.question: What is the Quantum Realm?
questions.explanation: The Quantum Realm is a subatomic dimension accessible by shrinking beyond atomic scale.

questions.question: Who is the God of Thunder in the MCU?
questions.explanation: Thor Odinson is the Asgardian God of Thunder and a founding Avenger.

questions.question: What is Captain Hook afraid of?
questions.explanation: Captain Hook fears the crocodile that swallowed his hand and a ticking clock.

questions.question: Who is the villain in The Emperor's New Groove?
questions.explanation: Yzma is the conniving advisor who wants to overthrow Emperor Kuzco.

questions.question: What does Gaston want in Beauty and the Beast?
questions.explanation: Gaston is obsessed with marrying Belle because she is the most beautiful girl in town.

questions.question: Who are Ursula's eel sidekicks in The Little Mermaid?
questions.explanation: Flotsam and Jetsam are Ursula's sneaky electric eel minions.

questions.question: What does Maleficent turn into at the end of Sleeping Beauty?
questions.explanation: Maleficent transforms into a fire-breathing dragon to fight Prince Phillip.

questions.question: Who is the villain in Pocahontas?
questions.explanation: Governor Ratcliffe leads the English settlers and greedily seeks gold in the New World.

questions.question: What is the name of Jafar's parrot?
questions.explanation: Iago is Jafar's loud and scheming parrot sidekick voiced by Gilbert Gottfried.

questions.question: Who is the villain in The Hunchback of Notre Dame?
questions.explanation: Judge Claude Frollo is the cruel minister who raises Quasimodo in the bell tower.

questions.question: What magic does Dr. Facilier use in The Princess and the Frog?
questions.explanation: Dr. Facilier, the Shadow Man, uses voodoo and makes deals with dark spirits.

questions.question: What is the Evil Queen's famous line in Snow White?
questions.explanation: The Evil Queen asks her magic mirror who is the fairest of them all.

questions.question: What animal is Prince John in Disney's Robin Hood?
questions.explanation: Prince John is a cowardly lion who rules England in King Richard's absence.

questions.question: What does Te Ka turn out to be in Moana?
questions.explanation: Te Ka is actually the goddess Te Fiti, corrupted after her heart was stolen.

questions.question: What is Shere Khan in The Jungle Book?
questions.explanation: Shere Khan is the fearsome Bengal tiger who wants to kill Mowgli.

questions.question: Who is Cruella de Vil's obsession in 101 Dalmatians?
questions.explanation: Cruella is obsessed with making a fur coat from dalmatian puppy spots.

questions.question: Who is the villain in Big Hero 6?
questions.explanation: Professor Callaghan is revealed to be the masked villain Yokai seeking revenge.

questions.question: Who is the villain in Wreck-It Ralph?
questions.explanation: King Candy is actually Turbo in disguise, a game-jumping villain.

questions.question: What are Pain and Panic in Hercules?
questions.explanation: Pain and Panic are Hades' bumbling imp sidekicks in Hercules.

questions.question: What does Rapunzel use as a weapon in Tangled?
questions.explanation: Rapunzel's trusty frying pan becomes her signature weapon throughout Tangled.

questions.question: Where is Moana from?
questions.explanation: Moana is from the fictional Polynesian island of Motunui.

questions.question: What is Tiana's best friend's name in The Princess and the Frog?
questions.explanation: Charlotte La Bouff is Tiana's wealthy and princess-obsessed best friend.

questions.question: What does Cinderella lose at the ball?
questions.explanation: Cinderella loses her glass slipper on the palace steps as she flees at midnight.

questions.question: Which Disney Princess is Scottish?
questions.explanation: Merida is the Scottish princess from Brave who is skilled with a bow and arrow.

questions.question: What animal does Jasmine have as a pet?
questions.explanation: Rajah is Princess Jasmine's loyal pet Bengal tiger.

questions.question: What is Aurora's other name in Sleeping Beauty?
questions.explanation: Aurora is raised in the forest under the name Briar Rose by three good fairies.

questions.question: Who are the three fairies in Sleeping Beauty?
questions.explanation: Flora, Fauna, and Merryweather are the three good fairies who raise Princess Aurora.

questions.question: What is Rapunzel's chameleon friend called?
questions.explanation: Pascal is Rapunzel's loyal chameleon companion in Tangled.

questions.question: Which princess can control ice and snow?
questions.explanation: Elsa has the magical power to create and control ice and snow.

questions.question: Who is Belle's father in Beauty and the Beast?
questions.explanation: Maurice is Belle's eccentric inventor father who gets lost in the forest.

questions.question: What does Ariel collect from the human world?
questions.explanation: Ariel has a secret grotto filled with human objects she calls gadgets and gizmos.

questions.question: Which princess disguises herself as a man to join the army?
questions.explanation: Mulan disguises herself as Ping to take her father's place in the Chinese army.

questions.question: What is the name of Pocahontas's love interest?
questions.explanation: Captain John Smith is the English explorer who falls in love with Pocahontas.

questions.question: In Moana, what must be returned to Te Fiti?
questions.explanation: Moana must return the heart of Te Fiti to stop the darkness spreading across the ocean.

questions.question: What is the name of Ariel's seagull friend?
questions.explanation: Scuttle is the seagull who claims to know about human objects but gets everything wrong.

questions.question: Which Disney Princess does not sing a solo in her movie?
questions.explanation: Merida from Brave is the only Disney Princess who does not have a solo song in her film.

questions.question: What movie features the song 'Be Our Guest'?
questions.explanation: Be Our Guest is sung by Lumiere and the enchanted objects in Beauty and the Beast.

questions.question: Who sings 'Part of Your World' in The Little Mermaid?
questions.explanation: Part of Your World is Ariel's signature song about wanting to live on land.

questions.question: What Disney song from Pinocchio became the Disney company's theme?
options: When You Wish Upon a Star
questions.explanation: When You Wish Upon a Star won an Oscar and became the Walt Disney Company's signature melody.

questions.question: Which Disney film features the song 'Hakuna Matata'?
questions.explanation: Hakuna Matata means 'no worries' and is sung by Timon and Pumbaa in The Lion King.

questions.question: Who sings 'We Don't Talk About Bruno' in Encanto?
questions.explanation: Multiple Madrigal family members each take turns singing about the taboo subject of Bruno.

questions.question: What movie features the song 'You've Got a Friend in Me'?
questions.explanation: You've Got a Friend in Me by Randy Newman is the iconic Toy Story theme song.

questions.question: Who wrote the music for Mary Poppins?
questions.explanation: Richard and Robert Sherman wrote Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious and all Mary Poppins songs.

questions.question: What Disney song includes the lyric 'tale as old as time'?
options: Can You Feel the Love Tonight
questions.explanation: Tale as old as time is the iconic opening lyric of the Beauty and the Beast waltz.

questions.question: Who wrote the songs for Tarzan?
questions.explanation: Phil Collins wrote and performed all songs for Tarzan including You'll Be in My Heart.

questions.question: What Moana song does Dwayne Johnson perform?
questions.explanation: Dwayne Johnson voices Maui and sings You're Welcome, written by Lin-Manuel Miranda.

questions.question: Which song is from Mulan?
options: I'll Make a Man Out of You
questions.explanation: I'll Make a Man Out of You is the training montage song sung by Captain Li Shang.

questions.question: What movie features 'Colors of the Wind'?
questions.explanation: Colors of the Wind is Pocahontas's signature song about respecting nature.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Can You Feel the Love Tonight' from The Lion King?
questions.explanation: Elton John composed the music and Tim Rice wrote the lyrics for The Lion King's songs.

questions.question: What is the opening song of Aladdin?
questions.explanation: Arabian Nights is the opening number sung by a merchant introducing the story of Aladdin.

questions.question: Which Frozen song became a worldwide phenomenon and won an Academy Award?
options: Do You Want to Build a Snowman
For the First Time in Forever
questions.explanation: Let It Go won the Academy Award for Best Original Song and became a cultural phenomenon.

questions.question: Which Disney Renaissance film does NOT have music by Alan Menken?
questions.explanation: The Lion King features music by Hans Zimmer with songs by Elton John and Tim Rice.

questions.question: What is Sebastian's full name in The Little Mermaid?
questions.explanation: Sebastian's full name is Horatio Thelonious Ignacious Crustaceous Sebastian.

questions.question: How many happy haunts are in the Haunted Mansion?
questions.explanation: The Haunted Mansion features 999 happy haunts with room for one more.

questions.question: What is EPCOT an acronym for?
options: Exciting Place for Children of Tomorrow
questions.explanation: EPCOT stands for Experimental Prototype Community of Tomorrow, Walt Disney's vision.

questions.question: Which Disney park has the Matterhorn Bobsleds?
questions.explanation: The Matterhorn Bobsleds at Disneyland was the world's first tubular steel roller coaster.

questions.question: What is the tallest mountain attraction at Walt Disney World?
questions.explanation: Expedition Everest at Animal Kingdom stands at 199 feet tall.

questions.question: Which Disneyland land features Sleeping Beauty Castle?
questions.explanation: Sleeping Beauty Castle is the gateway to Fantasyland at Disneyland.

questions.question: What Walt Disney World park is themed around movies?
questions.explanation: Disney's Hollywood Studios is themed around movies, TV, music, and theater.

questions.question: What is the Avatar-themed land called at Animal Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Pandora - The World of Avatar opened at Animal Kingdom in 2017.

questions.question: What ride lets you design and race your own car at EPCOT?
questions.explanation: Test Track at EPCOT lets guests design a virtual car and test it on a high-speed track.

questions.question: What is the pirate-themed ride at Disney parks called?
questions.explanation: Pirates of the Caribbean was one of the last attractions personally supervised by Walt Disney.

questions.question: Which Walt Disney World resort has the monorail running through it?
questions.explanation: The Contemporary Resort has the monorail running through its iconic A-frame building.

questions.question: What is the indoor roller coaster in Tomorrowland?
questions.explanation: Space Mountain is the classic indoor dark roller coaster in Tomorrowland.

questions.question: How many themed lands did Disneyland have on opening day in 1955?
questions.explanation: Disneyland opened with Main Street USA, Adventureland, Frontierland, Fantasyland, and Tomorrowland.

questions.question: What is the name of Disney's flagship merchandise store on Main Street?
questions.explanation: The Emporium is the large merchandise store on Main Street USA at Disney parks.

questions.question: What is the name of the nighttime show at EPCOT's World Showcase Lagoon?
questions.explanation: Luminous The Symphony of Us is the current nighttime spectacular at EPCOT.

questions.question: What ride replaced Splash Mountain at Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Tiana's Bayou Adventure replaced Splash Mountain, rethemed to The Princess and the Frog.

questions.question: What is the water coaster on the Disney Dream and Fantasy called?
questions.explanation: AquaDuck is the shipboard water coaster that extends over the edge of the ship.

questions.question: What is the teen club called on Disney cruise ships?
questions.explanation: Vibe is the exclusive teen hangout for ages 14-17 on Disney cruise ships.

questions.question: What is Disney Cruise Line's second private destination in the Bahamas?
questions.explanation: Lighthouse Point on the island of Eleuthera is Disney's second Bahamas destination.

questions.question: What celebration happens on deck as the ship departs?
questions.explanation: The Sailing Away party is a fun deck celebration with characters as the ship departs port.

questions.question: How many nights is the shortest regular Disney Cruise sailing?
questions.explanation: Three-night Bahamas cruises are the shortest regular Disney Cruise Line sailings.

questions.question: What is the main entertainment theater called on Disney cruise ships?
questions.explanation: The Walt Disney Theatre hosts Broadway-style stage shows on Disney cruise ships.

questions.question: What age group is the Edge club for on Disney ships?
questions.explanation: Edge is the tween hangout for ages 11-14 on Disney Cruise Line ships.

questions.question: What is the nursery for babies on Disney ships called?
questions.explanation: It's a Small World Nursery is available for infants and toddlers under age 3.

questions.question: Where does Disney Cruise Line primarily depart from in Florida?
questions.explanation: Port Canaveral is Disney Cruise Line's primary departure port in Florida.

questions.question: What is fish extender (FE) gifting on a Disney Cruise?
questions.explanation: Fish extender gifting is a fan tradition where passengers exchange small gifts hung on door hooks.

questions.question: What type of stateroom has a private outdoor balcony?
questions.explanation: Verandah staterooms feature a private balcony for enjoying ocean views.

questions.question: What is Disney Cruise Line's loyalty program called?
questions.explanation: Castaway Club rewards repeat cruisers with Silver, Gold, and Platinum membership levels.

questions.question: Which Disney ship introduced the AquaMouse water attraction?
questions.explanation: Disney Wish introduced AquaMouse, a family water attraction with animated show scenes.

questions.question: What year did the Disney Magic first set sail?
questions.explanation: Disney Magic launched in 1998 as the very first Disney Cruise Line ship.

questions.question: What is the name of the Disney Cruise Line's sixth ship?
questions.explanation: Disney Treasure is the sixth ship in the Disney Cruise Line fleet.

questions.question: What was Walt Disney's first theme park?
questions.explanation: Disneyland in Anaheim, California opened in 1955 as Walt's first theme park.

questions.question: What year did Walt Disney pass away?
questions.explanation: Walt Disney passed away on December 15, 1966 from lung cancer.

questions.question: What was the first cartoon with fully synchronized sound throughout?
questions.explanation: Steamboat Willie premiered in 1928 as the first cartoon with synchronized sound throughout.

questions.question: What company did Walt and Roy Disney originally found?
questions.explanation: Walt and Roy Disney founded the Disney Brothers Cartoon Studio in 1923.

questions.question: What small Missouri town inspired Main Street USA?
questions.explanation: Walt spent formative childhood years in Marceline, Missouri which inspired Main Street USA.

questions.question: How many competitive Academy Awards did Walt Disney win?
questions.explanation: Walt Disney won 22 competitive Academy Awards, more than any other individual in history.

questions.question: What was Disney's first fully live-action feature film?
questions.explanation: Treasure Island released in 1950 was Disney's first fully live-action feature film.

questions.question: What did Disney produce for the US military during World War II?
questions.explanation: Disney produced training and propaganda films for the US military during WWII.

questions.question: What TV show did Walt Disney personally host?
options: Walt Disney's Wonderful World of Color
questions.explanation: Walt hosted the anthology TV series which premiered in 1954 and ran for decades.

questions.question: Who was Walt Disney's wife?
questions.explanation: Lillian Bounds married Walt Disney in 1925 and famously suggested the name Mickey.

questions.question: What was Disney's first full-color animated short?
questions.explanation: Flowers and Trees (1932) was the first film in three-strip Technicolor and won an Oscar.

questions.question: What year did The Walt Disney Company acquire Pixar?
questions.explanation: Disney acquired Pixar in 2006 for approximately 7.4 billion dollars.

questions.question: What year did Disney acquire Marvel Entertainment?
questions.explanation: Disney acquired Marvel Entertainment in 2009 for approximately 4 billion dollars.

questions.question: What year did Disney acquire Lucasfilm?
questions.explanation: Disney acquired Lucasfilm and the Star Wars franchise in 2012 for 4.05 billion dollars.

questions.question: Who led Disney as CEO during the Renaissance era of the 1990s?
questions.explanation: Michael Eisner was CEO from 1984 to 2005 and oversaw the Disney Renaissance era.

questions.question: What year did the Disney Channel launch?
questions.explanation: The Disney Channel launched on April 18, 1983 as a premium cable channel.

questions.question: How many daughters did Walt Disney have?
questions.explanation: Walt had two daughters: Diane (biological) and Sharon (adopted).

questions.question: What US state was Walt Disney born in?
questions.explanation: Walt Disney was born in Chicago, Illinois on December 5, 1901.

questions.question: What was the first Disney feature to be entirely produced using CAPS digital technology?
questions.explanation: The Rescuers Down Under (1990) was the first feature entirely produced using CAPS technology.

questions.question: What year did The Lion King open on Broadway?
questions.explanation: The Lion King opened on Broadway on November 13, 1997 at the New Amsterdam Theatre.

questions.question: What Disney film was adapted into a Broadway show in 2018?
questions.explanation: Frozen opened on Broadway in March 2018 at the St. James Theatre.

questions.question: Who directed the Broadway production of The Lion King?
questions.explanation: Julie Taymor directed and designed the groundbreaking puppetry and staging.

questions.question: Which actor won a Tony Award for playing the Genie in Aladdin?
questions.explanation: James Monroe Iglehart won a Tony Award for his showstopping portrayal of the Genie.

questions.question: What song was written specifically for the Beauty and the Beast Broadway show?
questions.explanation: Home was written specifically for the Broadway production as Belle's Act 1 ballad.

questions.question: Which Disney Broadway show is set during the 1899 newsboys' strike?
questions.explanation: Newsies is set during the 1899 newsboys' strike against newspaper publishers in New York.

questions.question: What Disney Broadway show features songs by Phil Collins?
questions.explanation: Tarzan the musical features songs by Phil Collins from the animated film.

questions.question: How many Tony Awards did The Lion King win in 1998?
questions.explanation: The Lion King won 6 Tony Awards in 1998 including Best Musical and Best Direction.

questions.question: What Disney show features a magic carpet that flies over the audience?
questions.explanation: Aladdin's Broadway production features a magic carpet that soars over the audience.

questions.question: Which Disney Broadway show permanently closed after the COVID-19 shutdown?
questions.explanation: Frozen on Broadway permanently closed in 2020 after the COVID-19 shutdown.

questions.question: What is Ursula's big villain song in The Little Mermaid musical?
questions.explanation: Poor Unfortunate Souls is Ursula's show-stopping villain number.

questions.question: Which Disney Broadway show features the song 'Santa Fe'?
questions.explanation: Santa Fe is Jack Kelly's dream song about escaping to a better life out west.

questions.question: What was Julie Taymor's historic achievement at the Tony Awards?
options: First woman to win Best Direction of a Musical
questions.explanation: Julie Taymor was the first woman to win the Tony for Best Direction of a Musical.

questions.question: What type of puppets represent animals in The Lion King on Broadway?
questions.explanation: Julie Taymor designed innovative body puppets and masks that actors wear and manipulate.

questions.question: Which Disney Broadway show features 'Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious'?
questions.explanation: Mary Poppins the musical features the iconic tongue-twisting song from the 1964 film.

questions.question: Which Disney Broadway show features the song 'Carrying the Banner'?
questions.explanation: Carrying the Banner is the rousing opening ensemble number of Newsies.

questions.question: Which Disney film had both a Broadway musical AND a live-action remake first?
questions.explanation: Beauty and the Beast had a Broadway musical in 1994 and a live-action remake in 2017.

questions.question: What Disney Broadway show features an onstage beast-to-prince transformation?
questions.explanation: Beauty and the Beast features the dramatic transformation of the Beast back into the Prince.

questions.question: How long has The Lion King been running on Broadway (as of 2025)?
questions.explanation: The Lion King has been running since 1997 and is one of Broadway's longest-running shows.

questions.question: What year did Aladdin open on Broadway?
questions.explanation: Aladdin opened on Broadway in March 2014 and has been a hit ever since.

questions.question: What is the sit-down restaurant inside Cinderella Castle?
questions.explanation: Cinderella's Royal Table is the character dining restaurant inside Cinderella Castle.

questions.question: What foods come in Mickey's head shape at Disney parks?
options: All of the above and more
questions.explanation: Mickey-shaped foods include ice cream bars, waffles, pretzels, beignets, and rice krispie treats.

questions.question: Where can you get the famous Grey Stuff at Walt Disney World?
questions.explanation: The Grey Stuff is a cookies and cream mousse at Be Our Guest, inspired by the film's 'try the grey stuff, it's delicious.'

questions.question: What are the giant smoked snacks famous at Disney parks?
questions.explanation: Giant smoked turkey legs are an iconic Disney parks snack weighing about 1.5 pounds.

questions.question: What is the Polynesian-themed restaurant at Walt Disney World?
questions.explanation: 'Ohana at Disney's Polynesian Village Resort serves family-style Polynesian cuisine.

questions.question: What is the signature snack at the Main Street Confectionery?
questions.explanation: Hand-dipped candy and caramel apples are the signature treat at Disney confectionery shops.

questions.question: What is the popular wrap at Ronto Roasters in Galaxy's Edge?
questions.explanation: The Ronto Wrap is a pita filled with roasted pork and slaw, a popular Galaxy's Edge snack.

questions.question: What EPCOT restaurant lets you eat next to a giant aquarium?
questions.explanation: Coral Reef Restaurant at The Seas pavilion features dining alongside a massive aquarium.

questions.question: What restaurant at EPCOT simulates dining 220 miles above Earth?
questions.explanation: Space 220 simulates dining in space with virtual windows showing views of Earth below.

questions.question: Where can you find LeFou's Brew at Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: LeFou's Brew is a frozen apple juice drink topped with passion fruit foam at Gaston's Tavern.

questions.question: What is a Kitchen Sink sundae at Disney?
options: A dessert served in a mini kitchen sink
questions.explanation: The Kitchen Sink is a massive sundae served in a mini kitchen sink at Beaches & Cream.

questions.question: What Hollywood Studios restaurant has a drive-in movie theme?
questions.explanation: Sci-Fi Dine-In Theater seats guests in car-shaped booths watching retro B-movie clips.

questions.question: What famous restaurant sits at the top of the Contemporary Resort?
questions.explanation: California Grill on the 15th floor offers panoramic views of Magic Kingdom fireworks.

questions.question: What is the Tonga Toast at the Polynesian Resort?
questions.explanation: Tonga Toast is banana-stuffed sourdough French toast rolled in cinnamon sugar.

questions.question: What is Blue Milk at Galaxy's Edge made from?
questions.explanation: Blue Milk is a frozen plant-based blend with tropical flavors like dragon fruit and lime.

questions.question: What is the popular collectible snack container at Disney parks?
questions.explanation: Collectible popcorn buckets shaped like characters and attractions are hugely popular souvenirs.

questions.question: What unique snack is Tokyo Disneyland most famous for?
questions.explanation: Tokyo Disneyland is famous for its many unique popcorn flavors sold in collectible buckets.

questions.question: What is the School Bread from EPCOT's Norway pavilion?
questions.explanation: School Bread is a sweet cardamom bun filled with vanilla custard and topped with coconut.

questions.question: What is the Citrus Swirl flavor at Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Citrus Swirl is an orange soft serve treat at Sunshine Tree Terrace in Adventureland.

questions.question: What live-action Disney film features a magic nanny with an umbrella?
questions.explanation: Mary Poppins arrives with her magic umbrella and carpet bag in the 1964 classic.

questions.question: What is Disney's streaming service called?
questions.explanation: Disney+ launched on November 12, 2019 as Disney's dedicated streaming platform.

questions.question: In what movie does a wooden puppet wish to become a real boy?
questions.explanation: Pinocchio wishes upon a star to become a real boy in the 1940 Disney classic.

questions.question: What color are Mickey Mouse's shorts?
questions.explanation: Mickey Mouse's iconic outfit features red shorts with two white buttons.

questions.question: Who is Mickey Mouse's pet dog?
questions.explanation: Pluto is Mickey Mouse's loyal pet dog who first appeared in 1930.

questions.question: What is the name of Donald Duck's girlfriend?
questions.explanation: Daisy Duck is Donald's girlfriend who first appeared in 1940.

questions.question: What type of animal is Goofy?
questions.explanation: Goofy is an anthropomorphic dog, despite his pet-like friend Pluto also being a dog.

questions.question: What animal is Dumbo?
questions.explanation: Dumbo is a baby elephant with oversized ears that allow him to fly.

questions.question: Who is Goofy's son?
questions.explanation: Max Goof is Goofy's son, featured in A Goofy Movie and the TV show Goof Troop.

questions.question: What year did Disney+ launch?
questions.explanation: Disney+ launched on November 12, 2019 with over 10 million subscribers on day one.

questions.question: Which Pixar film features the line 'Just keep swimming'?
questions.explanation: Dory's motto 'Just keep swimming' is one of the most iconic lines from Finding Nemo.

questions.question: What is the Disney Vault?
options: Disney's practice of limiting home video releases
questions.explanation: The Disney Vault was the practice of periodically withdrawing films from sale to create demand.

questions.question: Who is Minnie Mouse's best friend?
questions.explanation: Daisy Duck is Minnie Mouse's best friend in the Disney universe.

questions.question: What Disney Channel show features twins living at a hotel?
options: The Suite Life of Zack & Cody
questions.explanation: Zack and Cody Martin live at the Tipton Hotel in this popular Disney Channel show.

questions.question: What hidden detail appears in nearly every Pixar film?
questions.explanation: The Pizza Planet truck from Toy Story appears as an Easter egg in nearly every Pixar film.

questions.question: What does 'Hakuna Matata' mean?
questions.explanation: Hakuna Matata is a Swahili phrase meaning 'no worries' popularized by The Lion King.

questions.question: Which Disney Renaissance film came first?
questions.explanation: The Little Mermaid (1989) is considered the start of the Disney Renaissance.

questions.question: What year did Disney acquire 21st Century Fox?
questions.explanation: Disney completed the acquisition of 21st Century Fox in March 2019 for 71.3 billion dollars.

questions.question: What is the hidden message in the dust cloud scene in The Lion King?
questions.explanation: The dust spells out SFX, a nod to the special effects team, despite popular urban legend.

questions.question: What is the name of the snowman Elsa creates in Frozen?
questions.explanation: Olaf is the friendly snowman that Elsa brings to life with her powers.

questions.question: In Wreck-It Ralph, what game does Ralph come from?
questions.explanation: Ralph is the villain in the arcade game Fix-It Felix Jr.

questions.question: What animal does Kuzco turn into in The Emperor's New Groove?
questions.explanation: Emperor Kuzco is turned into a llama by Yzma's potion.

questions.question: Who is the villain in The Rescuers?
questions.explanation: Madame Medusa kidnaps a young orphan girl to retrieve a diamond.

questions.question: In Big Hero 6, what does Baymax say when he activates?
options: Hello, I am Baymax, your personal healthcare companion
questions.explanation: Baymax always introduces himself as your personal healthcare companion.

questions.question: What is the name of the magical flower in Tangled?
questions.explanation: The Sundrop Flower has magical healing properties that Mother Gothel hoards.

questions.question: What is the name of the dinosaur toy in Toy Story?
questions.explanation: Rex is the anxious Tyrannosaurus Rex toy who is afraid of everything.

questions.question: In Onward, what are the brothers trying to do?
options: Bring back their father for one day
questions.explanation: Ian and Barley Lightfoot go on a quest to bring back their late father for 24 hours.

questions.question: What is the name of the fish tank gang leader in Finding Nemo?
questions.explanation: Gill is the Moorish Idol who leads the Tank Gang escape plans.

questions.question: In Toy Story 4, what new toy does Bonnie make?
questions.explanation: Forky is a spork craft project that Bonnie brings to life as a new toy.

questions.question: What is Sulley's full name in Monsters, Inc.?
questions.explanation: James P. Sullivan, known as Sulley, is the top scarer at Monsters, Inc.

questions.question: What sport does Riley play in Inside Out?
questions.explanation: Riley plays hockey and her love of the sport creates a core memory island.

questions.question: What is the name of Yoda's species?
questions.explanation: Yoda's species has never been officially named in Star Wars canon.

questions.question: What is the Rebel base in A New Hope?
questions.explanation: The Rebel base on Yavin 4 is where they launch the attack on the Death Star.

questions.question: Who trained Obi-Wan Kenobi?
questions.explanation: Qui-Gon Jinn was Obi-Wan's Jedi Master who was killed by Darth Maul.

questions.question: What is the name of Anakin Skywalker's mother?
questions.explanation: Shmi Skywalker raised Anakin alone on Tatooine as a slave.

questions.question: What does AT-AT stand for?
questions.explanation: AT-AT stands for All Terrain Armored Transport, the Empire's iconic walking tanks.

questions.question: Who built C-3PO?
questions.explanation: Young Anakin Skywalker built C-3PO from spare parts on Tatooine.

questions.question: What is the name of Thor's mischievous brother?
questions.explanation: Loki is the God of Mischief and Thor's adopted brother.

questions.question: What is Black Widow's real name?
questions.explanation: Natasha Romanoff is Black Widow, a former Russian spy and founding Avenger.

questions.question: Who is the main villain in Spider-Man: Homecoming?
questions.explanation: Adrian Toomes, the Vulture, is the villain played by Michael Keaton.

questions.question: Who becomes Captain America after Steve Rogers?
questions.explanation: Sam Wilson (Falcon) takes up the shield as the new Captain America.

questions.question: What planet is Thanos from?
questions.explanation: Thanos is from the planet Titan, which was devastated by overpopulation.

questions.question: What is the name of Wakanda's heart-shaped plant?
questions.explanation: The Heart-Shaped Herb grants the power of the Black Panther to its consumer.

questions.question: What is the name of Gaston's sidekick in Beauty and the Beast?
questions.explanation: LeFou is Gaston's loyal but bumbling sidekick.

questions.question: What is Syndrome's real name in The Incredibles?
questions.explanation: Buddy Pine was Mr. Incredible's rejected fan who became the villain Syndrome.

questions.question: What does Ursula transform into to trick Prince Eric?
questions.explanation: Ursula transforms into a beautiful woman named Vanessa using Ariel's stolen voice.

questions.question: Who is the villain in Mulan?
questions.explanation: Shan Yu is the ruthless leader of the Hun army who invades China.

questions.question: What animal is Kaa in The Jungle Book?
questions.explanation: Kaa is the hypnotic python who tries to eat Mowgli.

questions.question: Who is the villain in The Fox and the Hound?
questions.explanation: Amos Slade is the grumpy hunter who wants to kill Tod the fox.

questions.question: What is the name of Ariel's prince?
questions.explanation: Prince Eric is the human prince that Ariel falls in love with.

questions.question: What is the name of Rapunzel's kingdom in Tangled?
questions.explanation: Corona is the kingdom where Rapunzel was born as a princess.

questions.question: Who is Tiana's prince in The Princess and the Frog?
questions.explanation: Prince Naveen of Maldonia is turned into a frog by Dr. Facilier's voodoo.

questions.question: What is Cinderella's stepmother's name?
questions.explanation: Lady Tremaine is Cinderella's cold and cruel stepmother.

questions.question: What is the name of Belle's enchanted rose?
questions.explanation: The Enchanted Rose wilts as the Beast's deadline to break the curse approaches.

questions.question: What is the name of Snow White's prince?
questions.explanation: Snow White's prince is officially known simply as The Prince in the original film.

questions.question: What movie features 'I Just Can't Wait to Be King'?
questions.explanation: Young Simba sings I Just Can't Wait to Be King in The Lion King.

questions.question: Who sings 'Surface Pressure' in Encanto?
questions.explanation: Luisa sings Surface Pressure about the weight of expectations on her shoulders.

questions.question: What song does the Genie sing when first appearing in Aladdin?
questions.explanation: Friend Like Me is the Genie's big introductory number when freed from the lamp.

questions.question: Which movie features 'The Bare Necessities'?
questions.explanation: The Bare Necessities is sung by Baloo in The Jungle Book about enjoying a simple life.

questions.question: What Frozen 2 song is Elsa's signature power ballad?
questions.explanation: Into the Unknown is Elsa's power ballad from Frozen 2, nominated for an Oscar.

questions.question: Who sings 'Be Prepared' in The Lion King?
questions.explanation: Scar sings Be Prepared as he plots to overthrow Mufasa and take the throne.

questions.question: What ride lets you fly on a banshee at Animal Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Avatar Flight of Passage is a 3D flying simulator ride in Pandora.

questions.question: What is the name of the TRON ride at Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: TRON Lightcycle / Run opened at Magic Kingdom in 2023 in Tomorrowland.

questions.question: Which EPCOT country pavilion has a Frozen ride?
questions.explanation: Norway pavilion features Frozen Ever After, which replaced the original Maelstrom ride.

questions.question: What attraction lets you soar over world landmarks?
questions.explanation: Soarin' Around the World is a hang-gliding simulator over iconic landmarks.

questions.question: What dark ride takes you flying to Neverland?
questions.explanation: Peter Pan's Flight takes you flying over London and into Neverland.

questions.question: What roller coaster goes through Mount Everest at Animal Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Expedition Everest - Legend of the Forbidden Mountain features the terrifying Yeti.

questions.question: What is the name of the buffet restaurant on the Disney Dream and Fantasy?
questions.explanation: Cabanas is the casual buffet restaurant found on Disney Dream and Fantasy.

questions.question: What makes Disney the only cruise line to do this at sea?
questions.explanation: Disney Cruise Line is the only cruise line to set off fireworks at sea during Pirate Night.

questions.question: What Frozen-themed restaurant is on the Disney Wish?
questions.explanation: Arendelle: A Frozen Dining Adventure features singing and live entertainment during dinner.

questions.question: What color is the hull of Disney cruise ships?
questions.explanation: Disney cruise ships have a distinctive navy blue hull with red and yellow accents.

questions.question: What is the premium restaurant on the Disney Dream and Fantasy?
questions.explanation: Remy is the premium French-inspired dining experience named after the Ratatouille character.

questions.question: What Marvel-themed restaurant is on the Disney Wish?
questions.explanation: Worlds of Marvel is an interactive dining experience with Avengers-themed storytelling.

questions.question: What was the name of Walt Disney's first business that failed?
questions.explanation: Laugh-O-Gram Studio in Kansas City went bankrupt in 1923 before Walt moved to Hollywood.

questions.question: Who is the CEO of The Walt Disney Company as of 2025?
questions.explanation: Bob Iger returned as CEO of Disney in November 2022.

questions.question: What animated short won Disney's first Academy Award?
questions.explanation: Flowers and Trees (1932) won the first Academy Award for Best Animated Short Film.

questions.question: What year did Disneyland Paris open?
questions.explanation: Disneyland Paris (originally Euro Disney) opened on April 12, 1992.

questions.question: What year did Tokyo Disneyland open?
questions.explanation: Tokyo Disneyland opened on April 15, 1983 as the first Disney park outside the US.

questions.question: What Disney animated feature combined live-action and animation in 1944?
questions.explanation: The Three Caballeros (1944) was a groundbreaking blend of animation and live-action.

questions.question: What song from The Little Mermaid musical is Ariel's signature number?
questions.explanation: Part of Your World is Ariel's iconic I Want song in both the film and musical.

questions.question: Which Disney musical features the song 'King of New York'?
questions.explanation: King of New York is the celebratory Act 2 number in Newsies.

questions.question: Which Disney Broadway show won the Tony for Best Musical in 1998?
questions.explanation: The Lion King won the Tony Award for Best Musical in 1998.

questions.question: What Disney musical features the song 'Step in Time'?
questions.explanation: Step in Time is the chimney sweeps big dance number in Mary Poppins.

questions.question: What year did Beauty and the Beast close on Broadway?
questions.explanation: Beauty and the Beast ran for 13 years and closed in 2007 after 5,461 performances.

questions.question: What is Prince Hans' villain reveal song in Frozen the Musical?
questions.explanation: Hans of the Southern Isles is Prince Hans' villain reveal number in the stage show.

questions.question: What is the signature snack at Sleepy Hollow in Magic Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Sleepy Hollow is famous for its sweet and savory fresh waffle sandwiches.

questions.question: What character dining restaurant is at the Contemporary Resort?
questions.explanation: Chef Mickey's is a popular character dining buffet at the Contemporary Resort.

questions.question: What shape are Mickey Waffles?
questions.explanation: Mickey Waffles shaped like Mickey's head are a beloved Disney resort breakfast staple.

questions.question: What is the popular frozen drink at the France pavilion in EPCOT?
questions.explanation: The Grand Marnier Orange Slush is a cult favorite treat at EPCOT's France pavilion.

questions.question: What Marvel-themed dining is available at Disney parks?
questions.explanation: Pym Test Kitchen at Avengers Campus serves food in hilariously oversized and tiny portions.

questions.question: What is the iconic breakfast item at Disney resort hotels?
questions.explanation: Mickey-shaped waffles are the quintessential Disney resort breakfast item.

questions.question: What are the names of Cinderella's stepsisters?
questions.explanation: Anastasia and Drizella are Cinderella's mean stepsisters.

questions.question: What is the name of the toy store in Toy Story 2?
questions.explanation: Al's Toy Barn is the toy store owned by Al McWhiggin who steals Woody.

questions.question: Which Disney film was the first to have a theatrical sequel?
questions.explanation: The Rescuers Down Under (1990) was the first Disney animated theatrical sequel.

questions.question: What is the name of the villain queen in Snow White?
questions.explanation: The Evil Queen (Queen Grimhilde) is Snow White's vain stepmother.

questions.question: What year was the first Toy Story released?
questions.explanation: Toy Story released in 1995 as the first fully computer-animated feature film.

questions.question: What Disney character is known for losing a glass slipper?
questions.explanation: Cinderella famously loses her glass slipper at the stroke of midnight.
